issue_summary: allow flushing by ram, doc count, or both (whichever comes first) in IndexWriter
javadoc_before:
/** Determines the minimal number of documents required * before the buffered in-memory documents are flushed as * a new Segment. Large values generally gives faster * indexing. * *

When this is set, the writer will flush every * maxBufferedDocs added documents and never flush by RAM * usage.

* *

The default value is 0 (writer flushes by RAM * usage).

* * @throws IllegalArgumentException if maxBufferedDocs is * smaller than 2 * @see #setRAMBufferSizeMB */
javadoc_after:
/** Determines the minimal number of documents required * before the buffered in-memory documents are flushed as * a new Segment. Large values generally gives faster * indexing. * *

When this is set, the writer will flush every * maxBufferedDocs added documents. Pass in {@link * #DISABLE_AUTO_FLUSH} to prevent triggering a flush due * to number of buffered documents. Note that if flushing * by RAM usage is also enabled, then the flush will be * triggered by whichever comes first.

* *

Disabled by default (writer flushes by RAM usage).

* * @throws IllegalArgumentException if maxBufferedDocs is * enabled but smaller than 2, or it disables maxBufferedDocs * when ramBufferSize is already disabled * @see #setRAMBufferSizeMB */
